# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - install
  - deploy-to-qa-cluster
  - deploy-to-prod-cluster
variables:
  APP_NAME: demo
  REGISTRY_NAMESPACE: $REGISTRY_NS
  BUILD_TAG: "1.0.$CI_PIPELINE_IID"  # the unique pipeline id, 1.0.24678354
install-tools:
  stage: install
  script:
    - apk update && apk add jq
    - apk --no-cache add ruby
    - gem install k8s-openapi
    - gem install kubeclient
    - gem install openshift  # for the gitlab operator
    - pip3 install kubernetes
    - mkdir /app
    - echo "export REGISTRY_NAMESPACE=registry-ns" >> $BASH_ENV
    - echo "$APP_NAME-$BUILD_TAG.tag" > $PROJECT_DIR/.version
deploy-to-qa-cluster:
  stage: deploy-to-qa-cluster
  needs: install
  script: 
    - cd $PROJECT_DIR && ./bin/deploy.sh $REGISTRY_NAMESPACE
    - echo "$REGISTRY_NS" > $PROJECT_DIR/$APP_NAME-$BUILD_TAG.tag
deploy-to-prod-cluster: 
  stage: deploy-to-prod-cluster
  needs: [install, deploy-to-qa-cluster]
  when: on_success # only run this pipeline if the pipeline that ran after the deploy-to-prod fails
  script: 
    - cd $PROJECT_DIR
    - echo "$REGISTRY_NAMESPACE" > $PROJECT_DIR/$APP_NAME-$BUILD_TAG.tag
